evidence.snippet: Premier Manufacturer of Electromechanical Actuators • Volz Servos
With more than 40 years of experience in air mobility, Volz is best known as a leading manufacturer of electromechanical actuators for aerial vehicles.

items.notes: Volz's core product is electromechanical servo actuators — custom electromechanical mechanisms for UAV/AAM flight control surfaces, tilting mechanisms, landing gear, and propeller pitch

evidence.snippet: Certifiable Flight Control Actuators for AAM & eVTOL • Volz Servos
Advanced air mobility (AAM), unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and even remotely operated vehicles (ROVs): Our technology controls it.

items.notes: Actuators explicitly branded as 'Certifiable Flight Control Actuators' for UAV and AAM; used in control surfaces, VTOL tilting, and propeller pitch control

evidence.snippet:
**Volz Servos GmbH**
Heinrich-Krumm-Strasse 5
63073 Offenbach am Main / Germany
With more than 40 years of experience in air mobility, Volz is best known as a leading manufacturer of electromechanical actuators for aerial vehicles.
Volz servos are the technological standard for controlling UAVs due to their unbeaten reliability.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Supplies servo actuators as components to drone OEMs such as BETA Technologies, Dufour Aerospace, Quantum Systems, Zipline, Schiebel, and Insitu

items.notes: Used in Dufour Aerospace tilt-wing project; minimized installation space and weight

evidence.snippet: Drones, for example, are now capable of revolutionizing both local and supra-regional transport systems. With our know-how, we provide proven technology that can also easily go into series production.
Volz for fly-by-wire aircraft system: Volz Servos has signed a production agreement with BETA Technologies as an electromechanical actuator partner for the ALIA project.

tagline: German-made electromechanical servo actuators (13–30 mm class) for UAV flight control, throttle management, and redundant systems. Production-ready across standard, high-torque, and submersible variants; integrated with BAE Systems, Airbus, Thales, and emerging AAM platforms. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status not publicly confirmed. No major U.S. domestic supply-chain presence documented.
